data.title: 30-Year Treasury Yield Rises to 4.751% — Data Talk
data.description:
The 30-year yield rose 0.010 percentage point to 4.751% today. The price fell 5/32 to 98.

  • Yield is up for two consecutive trading days
  • Yield is up 0.016 percentage point over the last two trading days
  • Largest two-day yield gain since Monday, Nov. 17, 2025
  • Yield is up six of the past eight trading days
  • Highest yield since Monday, Oct. 6, 2025,
  • Yield is off 0.337 percentage point from its 52-week high of 5.089% hit Wednesday, May 21, 2025
  • Yield is up 0.421 percentage point from its 52-week low of 4.330% hit Friday, Dec. 6, 2024
  • Yield is up 0.159 percentage point from 52 weeks ago
  • Yield is up 0.360 percentage point from its 2025 closing low of 4.391% hit Friday, April 4, 2025
  • Month-to-date the yield is up 0.082 percentage point
  • Year-to-date the yield is down 0.034 percentage point

Data based on 3 p.m. ET values

Source: Tradeweb FTSE U.S. Treasury Closing Prices

data.description:

data.title: PLATTS: 100--Reactor pressure vessel installed at Egypt's El Dabaa-1 nuclear unit: Rosatom
data.description: (Platts)--19Nov2025/341 pm EST/2041 GMT **Key stage for Africa's first Gen III+ reactor construction **Rosatom targets commercial operation in 2028 Rosatom installed the reactor pressure vessel for the 1,200-MW El Dabaa-1 nuclear Nov. 19, the company said in a statement on its website. The equipment manufactured by Rosatom's machine-building division was delivered to the construction site Oct. 21, after which it passed an inspection. The RPV's installation is the key stage in the construction of El Dabaa-1, Africa's first generation III+ reactor, Rosatom CEO Alexey Likhachev said in the statement. The planned launch of four reactors at the El Dabaa nuclear power plant, with a total capacity of 4.8 GW, will substantially strengthen Egypt's energy security, Russian President Vladimir Putin said Nov. 19, according to the Kremlin's website. "Our Generation III[+] reactors will produce up to 37 billion kWh a year, generating approximately 10% of electricity in the country," said Putin. Rosatom is helping Egypt gain experience in the nuclear field, he said, estimating that around 55% of contract and subcontract orders have been performed by Egyptian firms. "Russia will continue to provide assistance to its Egyptian partners at all vital stages of the nuclear project. This includes long-term supplies of reactor fuel, the technical maintenance of the plant, and spent nuclear fuel management," the Russian president added. Putin said that Rosatom is prepared to share its unique technologies with Egypt, specifically in the areas of small modular reactor assembly and the application of nuclear energy in medicine and agriculture. Egyptian President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi also noted the importance of the plant construction for the Egyptian economy. "In line with Egypt Vision 2030, this event confirms Egypt's role as a regional energy hub and aligns with our efforts to achieve comprehensive development," el-Sisi said, according to the Kremlin website. The nuclear plant "represents a turning point in the localisation of knowledge and a genuine investment in our national human resources." Rosatom plans to put El Dabaa-1 into commercial operation in 2028, the company said in 2024. No information is currently available on the start-up schedule of other units. The 4.8-GW plant, which will comprise four Russian-designed VVER-1200s, is being built under an intergovernmental agreement signed in 2015. — Vladislav Vorotnikov, ci.support@spglobal.com-- Edited by Meghan Gordon, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

data.title: PLATTS: 1107--Platts Americas Wind and Solar Renewable Capture Price Indexes Daily Commentary - 19Nov25
data.description: (Platts)--19Nov2025/337 pm EST/2037 GMT **Peakload demand levels rise across board **CAISO capture rates surge $8.50 on average Renewable capture prices across the US Regional Transmission Organizations and Independent System Operators strengthened Nov. 17 as the workweek began, tracking demand fundamentals across most regions. CAISO The California ISO saw solar capture prices rose around 32%, or around $10 on average, Nov. 17, while wind capture rates increased 15%, or about $6. CAISO's average regional temperatures increased by 3.8 F to 74.7 F, according to CustomWeather. Peakload demand within the same period rose 6.9%, to 27.02 GW. MISO, NYISO The Midcontinent ISO saw its five renewable hubs' capture rates increase Nov. 17, with average rises across the MISO footprint of around 20%, and the Minnesota solar hub seeing a negative change day over day. From Nov. 17, MISO saw peakload demand rise 7.2% to 80.45 GW. Average temperatures across the MISO footprint were 2.3% lower at 40 F. The New York ISO saw higher capture prices, with Hudson Valley and West Hub wind averaging 29% higher. Here, peakload demand gained 9.2% on the day to 15.07 GW, as the average temperature dipped 6.2 F to 39.1 F. — Daryna Kotenko, daryna.kotenko@spglobal.com-- Edited by Jim Levesque, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

data.title: Fed Leans Toward December Rate Pause, Minutes Show — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Nicole Goodkind

Many Federal Reserve officials said it would likely be appropriate to keep interest rates steady for the rest of year, though they expressed concerns over a weakening labor market at their latest policy meeting.

Minutes from the central bank's Oct. 28-29 meeting show a committee wrestling with conflicting economic signals and struggling to reach consensus on which is the most pressing issue — stubborn inflation or a labor market showing signs of distress. Officials lowered interest rates for the second time this year by a quarter percentage point to between 3.75% and 4.00% at last month's meeting of the Federal Open Market Committee.

In the minutes, the majority of officials agreed that an October rate cut was necessary in light of recent lackluster employment data. It was the path forward that created disagreements.

In the minutes, the majority of officials agreed that an October rate cut was necessary in light of recent lackluster employment data. It was the path forward that created disagreements.

Many participants "suggested that, under their economic outlooks, it would likely be appropriate to keep the target range unchanged for the rest of the year," read the meeting notes, released Wednesday afternoon.

In Fed speak, "many" typically trumps "several," indicating that a slight majority of Fed officials thought pausing would be appropriate in December. Still, it should be noted that not all of those participants are voting members.

Meanwhile, several policymakers "assessed that a further lowering of the target range for the federal-funds rate could well be appropriate in December if the economy evolved about as they expected over the coming intermeeting period," according to the minutes.

Still, all participants said they agreed that "that monetary policy was not on a preset course and would be informed by a wide range of incoming data, the evolving economic outlook, and the balance of risks."

Fed officials also acknowledged that cutting interest rates further could risk a miscommunication around their commitment to lowering inflation to its 2% goal.

Many participants, "remarked that overall inflation had been above target for some time and had shown little sign of returning sustainably to the 2 percent objective in a timely manner," according to the minutes.

The latest official consumer price index reading, for September, showed that inflation is at its highest since January and has remained above the Fed's 2% goal for nearly five years.

The minutes, wrote Joseph Brusuelas of RSM "provided unmistakable evidence of a split between hawks and doves with text implying a slight tilt towards the hawks that made a persuasive point around the risk of higher inflation becoming entrenched."

Of course, the economic backdrop has changed since October. The government shutdown has ended, and key data releases, including employment and inflation, are expected to resume eventually.

On Wednesday, the Department of Labor said it wouldn't release the October unemployment rate, and that it would delay the release of October and November payrolls data to Dec. 16, after the final Fed meeting of the year.

That delay, said Brusuelas, "means the fog of uncertainty for the Fed will have only partially eased. Under such conditions the Fed will not and should not cut rates."

Investors are now pricing in a 31.6% chance of a rate cut at the December meeting, down from 50.1% a day ago, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

Fed officials also issued a warning about artificial intelligence investments. Several participants highlighted "the possibility of a disorderly fall in equity prices, especially in the event of an abrupt reassessment of the possibilities of AI-related technology," the minutes read.

Write to Nicole Goodkind at nicole.goodkind@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: AECOM Down Over 9%, On Track for Largest Percent Decrease Since March 2020 — Data Talk
data.description:
AECOM (ACM) is currently at $114.95, down $12.19 or 9.59%

  • Would be lowest close since Aug. 4, 2025, when it closed at $111.94
  • On pace for largest percent decrease since March 18, 2020, when it fell 14.72%
  • Currently down four consecutive days; down 14.09% over this period
  • Longest losing streak since April 8, 2025, when it fell for four straight trading days
  • Worst four day stretch since the four days ending June 11, 2020, when it fell 15.47%
  • Down 14.44% month-to-date
  • Up 7.61% year-to-date
  • Down 14.44% from its all-time closing high of $134.35 on Oct. 31, 2025
  • Up 5.01% from 52 weeks ago (Nov. 20, 2024), when it closed at $109.47
  • Down 14.44% from its 52-week closing high of $134.35 on Oct. 31, 2025
  • Up 31.84% from its 52-week closing low of $87.19 on April 8, 2025
  • Traded as low as $114.65; lowest intraday level since Aug. 5, 2025, when it hit $114.46
  • Down 9.82% at today's intraday low; largest intraday percent decrease since April 1, 2020, when it fell as much as 10.35%

All data as of 3:31:56 PM ET

Source: Dow Jones Market Data, FactSet

data.title: ATM: Record sales, supply chain transformation, and a $300M special dividend headline the AGM
data.description:
Record FY25 sales and profit growth were driven by strong execution and innovation, with new product launches and supply chain transformation. The Board proposed a $300 million special dividend, new director appointments, and continued focus on sustainability and expansion in Asia.

Original document: a2 Milk Company Limited [ATM] Slides Release — Nov. 19 2025

data.title: Wheat Futures Slide as Winter Crop Gets Better Weather — Daily Grain Highlights
data.description:
By Kirk Maltais

  • Wheat for March delivery fell 1.7% to $5.48 3/4 a bushel on the Chicago Board of Trade Wednesday, as winter wheat in the center of the U.S. is getting more supportive weather.
  • Soybeans for January delivery fell 1.6% to $11.35 a bushel.
  • Corn for December delivery fell 1.6% to $4.30 a bushel.

HIGHLIGHTS

Rainy Days: Winter wheat growing in the Plains is getting rainfall over the course of the week, which is welcomed by growers.

"U.S. forecasts have rains for the winter wheat areas over the next week, with good moisture for the new crop winters," said Charlie Sernatinger of Marex in a note.

In a report published Wednesday, analysts with S&P Global Energy pegged the U.S. winter wheat crop at 32.4 million acres, which is slightly lower than the 33.2 million acres seen last year.

Hitting the Brakes: Plans from the Trump administration to limit the amount of feedstocks being imported into the U.S. for production of biofuels may be delayed by one to two years, according to Reuters. Concerns about how enforcing that rule could raise fuel prices are behind the potential delay. This sent futures lower, with soybeans in particular coming under pressure as soyoil and soymeal fell by more than 2%.

INSIGHT

Back to Back: The USDA confirmed a new sale of soybean exports to China, this after 792,000 metric tons were sold on Tuesday. On Wednesday, 330,000 metric tons were sold for delivery in the 2025-26 marketing year.

Sales to China are supportive for grain futures, but traders remain cautious about pinning their hopes on that target actually being met.

"We got a ways to go to hit that 12 mmt," said Naomi Blohm of Total Farm Marketing in a note.

Improving Situation: Weather in Argentina is improving for farmers, with a drying out taking place that will allow for those farmers to get back into their fields to plant soybeans.

"A near-term drier forecast is in place for much of Argentina, which will be welcome after excessive rains and flooding have impacted the ability to plant this year's soybean crop in parts of Buenos Aires," said Randy Mittelstaedt of RJO Futures in a note.

The country's agricultural agency says that only 12% of soybean acres have been planted as of last week, versus an average pace of 22%.

Missing Some Input: The U.S. labor data blackout will extend beyond the next Fed meeting, increasing uncertainty and raising the odds of a pause on interest-rate cuts.

The Bureau of Labor Statistics is set to publish September payrolls Thursday, but it says the next publication won't be until Dec. 16, when the full November data set will include incomplete October figures.

The Fed meeting will conclude on Dec. 10. Minutes from the central bank's October meeting show a high degree of disagreement among officials about whether a third consecutive cut would be warranted next month. How this shakes out looks to affect the direction seen in commodity futures.

AHEAD

  • The USDA is due to release its weekly export sales report at 8:30 a.m. EST Thursday.
  • The USDA is scheduled to release its monthly Cattle on Feed Report at 3 p.m. EST Friday.
  • The USDA is due to release its weekly grain export inspections report at 11 a.m. EST Monday.
  • Paulo Trevisani contributed to this article.

Write to Kirk Maltais at kirk.maltais@wsj.com

data.title: JPMorgan Likes Upside at BellRing Brands Despite 4Q Earnings Miss — Market Talk
data.description: J.P. Morgan analysts still see upside for BellRing Brands despite fourth-quarter earnings and full-year guidance that came in lower than Wall Street's expectations for the maker of protein drinks and other nutrition products. Management gave some reasons for optimism during Tuesday's earnings call, analysts wrote in a note, including greater placement for its ready-to-drink protein shakes at a key mass customer it believes to be Walmart. Analysts also expect distribution growth at grocery stores to continue at 2025 levels, while placement is expected to improve due to a new broker relationship. "We still think the risk-reward skews to the upside," they wrote. The stock is up 10% after falling following Tuesday's earnings release. (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)
